blocks:
Surah Al-Jumu‘ah

Medinan, and it consists of eleven verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ الْمَلِكِ الْقُدُّوسِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ. (1) Whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth glorifies Allah, the Sovereign, the All-holy, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (٢) هُوَ الَّذِي بَعَثَ فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ those who do not possess the Book رَسُولاً مِنْهُمْ يَتْلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ آياتِهِ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ from the impurities (خبائث) of beliefs (العقائد) and sincerity (الإخلاص) وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ the Quran and the Law (الشريعة) وَإِنْ And indeed they كانُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ لَفِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ from polytheism (الشرك) and the impurity (خبث) of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهليّة). In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ ** (to the unlettered people). He said: "They used to write, but they did not possess a Book from Allah, nor had a messenger been sent to them, so Allah attributed them to the unlettered people (الأميّين)." And in al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Jawad (peace be upon him): He was asked why the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his Household) was named al-Ummi (الأمّي). He asked: "What do the people say?" It was said: "They claim that He was only named al-Ummi because He did not know how to write." He said: "They have lied, may the curse of Allah be upon them! How could that be, when Allah says: هُوَ الَّذِي بَعَثَ فِي الْأُمِّيِّينَ رَسُولاً مِنْهُمْ يَتْلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ آياتِهِ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ ** (It is He who sent to the unlettered people a messenger from among themselves, to recite to them His signs, and to purify them, and to teach them the Book and wisdom). How could He teach them what He Himself did not master? By Allah, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was able to read and write in seventy-two, or He said seventy-three, languages. He was only named al-Ummi (الأمّي) because He was from the people of Mecca (مكّة), and Mecca is one of the Mothers of the Towns (أمّهات القرى). That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: لِتُنْذِرَ أُمَّ الْقُرى وَمَنْ حَوْلَها (that you may warn the Mother of the Towns and those around it)." This narration has already passed in Surah Al-A‘raf.

(٣) وَآخَرِينَ مِنْهُمْ لَمَّا يَلْحَقُوا بِهِمْ ** (3) and others of them who have not yet joined them. They have not yet joined them, but they will join them. It is said that they are those who came after the Companions (الصحابة) until the Day of Judgment, for his call and his teaching encompass everyone. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are the non-Arabs (الأعاجم) and those who do not speak the language of the Arabs (العرب). He said: And it was narrated that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited this verse, and it was said to Him: "Who are these?" So He placed His hand on the shoulder of Salman and said: "If faith (الإيمان) were in the Pleiades (الثّريا), men from these would surely attain it." ** وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ. and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise.

(٤) ذلِكَ فَضْلُ اللهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (4) That is Allah’s grace, which He grants to whomever He wishes, and Allah is the Possessor of the great grace. which makes the blessings of the world and the bliss of the Hereafter insignificant before it. (٥) مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ حُمِّلُوا التَّوْراةَ ** (5) The parable of those who were charged with the Torah, yet did not bear it, is that of an ass carrying volumes [of books]. They learned it and were obligated to act upon it, ثُمَّ لَمْ يَحْمِلُوها ** they did not act upon it and did not benefit from what was in it, كَمَثَلِ الْحِمارِ يَحْمِلُ أَسْفاراً [is] like the example of the donkey carrying volumes (كُتُب) of knowledge; it tires itself in carrying them but does not benefit from them. القمّيّ said: The donkey carries the books (الكتب) but does not know what is in them nor does it act upon them. Likewise, the Children of Israel (بنو إسرائيل) have carried [it] like the donkey (الحمار), and they do not know what is in it nor do they act upon it. بِئْسَ مَثَلُ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ.** Evil is the example of the people who deny Allah’s signs, and Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot.

(٦) قُلْ يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ هادُوا ** (6) Say, 'O you who are Judaized, (meaning: became Jews) إِنْ زَعَمْتُمْ أَنَّكُمْ أَوْلِياءُ لِلَّهِ مِنْ دُونِ النَّاسِ ** if you claim that you are Allah's friends, to the exclusion of other people, (since they used to say: "We are the friends of Allah and His beloved ones") فَتَمَنَّوُا الْمَوْتَ ** then long for death, (meaning: then long for Allah to cause you to die and transfer you from the abode of affliction (دار البليّة) to the abode of honor (دار الكرامة)). Al-Qummi said: Indeed, it is written in the Torah that the friends of Allah (أولياء الله) long for death. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** should you be truthful.' (in your claim.)

(٧) وَلا يَتَمَنَّوْنَهُ أَبَداً بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (7) And they will never wish for it because of what their hands have sent ahead. because of what they sent forth of disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالظَّالِمِينَ and Allah knows best the wrongdoers. The complete exegesis (تفسير) of that preceded in Surat al-Baqarah.

(٨) قُلْ إِنَّ الْمَوْتَ الَّذِي تَفِرُّونَ مِنْهُ ** (8) Say, 'Indeed the death from which you flee and you fear that you might wish for it with your tongues, fearing that it might afflict you, so that you would be seized for your deeds. فَإِنَّهُ مُلاقِيكُمْ** will indeed meet you. You will not escape it; it is surely reaching you.

In al-Qummi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), He said: "O people, every person will meet in his flight that from which he flees, and the appointed term (الأجل) is the driving of the soul toward it, and fleeing from it is its encounter (موافاتُه)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "The years are counted, then the months are counted, then the days are counted, then the hours are counted, then the breath (النفس) is counted." فَإِذا جاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ساعَةً وَلا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (When their term comes, they will not defer it by a single hour nor advance it.) ** ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلى عالِمِ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ** Then you will be returned to the Knower of the sensible and the unseen, and He will inform you concerning what you used to do.' by recompensing you for it.

(٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلاةِ meaning: the call (أَذَّنَ) is made for it مِنْ يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ It was said: It was named thus because of the gathering (اجتماع) of people therein for the prayer. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah gathered His creation therein for the guardianship (وِلايَة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and his successor (وَصِيّ) in the Covenant (المِيثَاق), so He named it the Day of Gathering (يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ) because He gathered His creation therein." ** فَاسْعَوْا إِلى ذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (hasten to the remembrance of Allah) meaning: to the prayer, as is understood from what precedes it and what follows it. It was said: meaning, proceed to it with deliberate haste (مُسْرِعِينَ قَصْداً), for السَّعْي (hastening) is less than الْعَدْو (running). In al-Majma': 'Abd Allah ibn Mas'ud read it as: فَامْضُوا إِلَى ذِكْرِ اللهِ (then proceed to the remembrance of Allah). He said: And this was narrated from the Commander of the Faithful, al-Baqir, and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them). And al-Qummi said: It is hastening (الإِسْرَاع) in walking. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: فَاسْعَوْا ** (hasten), meaning: proceed (امْضُوا). And in al-'Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The meaning of فَاسْعَوْا ** (hasten) is turning back (الِانْكِفَاء). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: فَاسْعَوْا إِلى ذِكْرِ اللهِ ** (hasten to the remembrance of Allah). He said: "Act and be quick, for it is a day made difficult (مُضَيَّق) for the Muslims. The reward for the deeds of the Muslims therein is commensurate with the difficulty imposed upon them, and the good deed (الحَسَنَة) and the evil deed (السَّيِّئَة) are multiplied therein." He said: "By Allah, it has reached me that the companions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله were preparing themselves for Friday on Thursday because it is a day made difficult (مُضَيَّق) for the Muslims." ** وَذَرُوا الْبَيْعَ ** (and leave all business.) and abandon transaction/dealing. In al-Faqih: It is narrated that in Medina, when the caller (المُؤَذِّن) called the adhan on Friday, a caller (مُنَادٍ) would announce: "Sale is forbidden (حُرِّمَ الْبَيْع), sale is forbidden (حُرِّمَ الْبَيْع)." ** ذلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ ** (That is better for you,) meaning: hastening to the remembrance of Allah is better for you than transaction, for the benefit of the Hereafter is better and more lasting. ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ ** (if you know.) good and evil. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Allah has obligated upon the people thirty-five prayers from one Friday to the next. Among them is one prayer that Allah obligated in congregation (جَمَاعَة), which is the Friday prayer (الجُمُعَة). And He lifted the obligation from nine: the minor (الصَّغِير), the elderly (الكَبِير), the insane (المَجْنُون), the traveler (المُسَافِر), the slave (العَبْد), the woman (المَرْأَة), the sick (المَرِيض), the blind (الأَعْمَى), and whoever is at the distance of two farsakhs (فَرْسَخَيْن)." In al-Tahdhib and al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Upon whom is the Friday prayer obligatory (تَجِبُ)?" He said: "It is obligatory upon seven individuals (نَفَر) from the Muslims. And there is no Friday prayer for fewer than five Muslims, one of whom is the Imam. So, if seven gather and they have no fear, one of them leads them in prayer (أَمَّهُمْ) and delivers the sermon (خَطَبَهُمْ)." I say: Perhaps the intended meaning is that it is obligatory upon seven as a certainty (حَتْماً) and a firm resolve (عَزِيمَة), without any license (رُخْصَة) to abandon it. And it is obligatory for five as an option (تَخْيِيراً) and as the preferred course, with license to abandon it. By this interpretation, the differing narrations regarding five and seven are reconciled. This is supported by the fact that the obligation (الوُجُوب) is conveyed using the particle لَام (لِ) for five, and the particle عَلَى for seven. As for when they are fewer than five, the Friday prayer is neither obligatory upon them nor permissible for them; rather, it is mandatory (حَتْماً) for them to pray four units (أَرْبَعاً). And the narrations concerning the obligation of the Friday prayer are too numerous to count (أَكْثَرُ مِنْ أَنْ تُحْصَى).

(10) فَإِذا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلاةُ ** (And when the prayer is finished) — meaning, it has been performed and finished — ** فَانْتَشِرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اللهِ. (disperse in the land, and seek Allah’s grace.)

In al-Majma' and al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The prayer is on Friday, and the dispersal (الانتشار) is on Saturday.

And in al-Uyun and al-Qummi: that which is similar in meaning.

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Indeed, I ride out for a need which Allah has already sufficed, yet I only ride out seeking that Allah may see me spending the morning in the pursuit of lawful provision (الحلال). Do you not hear the saying of Allah, exalted is His Name: فَإِذا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلاةُ فَانْتَشِرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اللهِ. (And when the prayer is finished, disperse in the land, and seek Allah’s grace.)"

And by the narration of Anas, from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: وَابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِ اللهِ ** (and seek Allah’s grace) is not seeking worldly matters (دنيا), but rather visiting the sick, attending a funeral, and visiting a brother in Allah.

** وَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ كَثِيراً ** (and remember Allah greatly) — and remember Allah in all your states (مجامع أحوالكم) and do not restrict His remembrance to the prayer.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "Whoever remembers Allah sincerely in the marketplace when people are heedless and preoccupied with what they are engaged in, Allah writes for him a thousand good deeds, and Allah forgives him on the Day of Resurrection with a forgiveness that has not occurred to the heart of any human."

** لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ ** (so that you may be felicitous) — by the good of the two abodes (الدّارين).

(١١) وَإِذا رَأَوْا تِجارَةً أَوْ لَهْواً انْفَضُّوا إِلَيْها ** (11) And when they sight a bargain or a diversion, they scatter towards it, They scattered towards it (انصرفوا إليها). Thus it is in al-Majma'.

And al-Qummi narrates from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): وَتَرَكُوكَ قائِماً ** (and leave you standing) meaning, delivering the sermon (تخطب) upon the pulpit (على المنبر). Thus they both narrated it. قُلْ ما عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (Say: 'What is with Allah) meaning, of the reward (من الثواب) خَيْرٌ مِنَ اللهْوِ وَمِنَ التِّجارَةِ ** (is better than diversion and bargain,') for that is confirmed (محقّق) and eternal (مخلّد), contrary to what you imagine (تتوهّمون) regarding the benefit of the two.

Al-Qummi narrates from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed: خَيْرٌ مِنَ اللهْوِ وَمِنَ التِّجارَةِ ** (is better than diversion and bargain,) for those who are Godwary (للّذين اتّقوا).

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He used to recite: خَيْرٌ مِنَ اللهْوِ وَمِنَ التِّجارَةِ ** (is better than diversion and bargain,) for those who are Godwary (للّذين اتقوا) ** وَاللهُ خَيْرُ الرَّازِقِينَ ** (and Allah is the best of providers.') So rely upon Him (فتوكّلوا عليه) and seek provision (الرّزق) from Him. Al-Qummi said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was praying with the people on Friday, when a caravan (ميرة) entered, and before it were people beating drums (بالدّفوف) and playing musical instruments (والملاهي). So the people abandoned the prayer and went to look at them, and Allah revealed [this Ayah].

In al-Majma', narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah, he said: A caravan (عير) approached while we were praying with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the people scattered towards it (فانفضّ النّاس إليها). No one remained except twelve men, and I was among them. So the Ayah was revealed. In one narration, he صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, had you followed one another until none of you remained, the valley would have flowed with fire for you."

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is obligatory (الواجب) upon every believer, if he is one of our followers (شيعة), that he recite al-Jumu'ah and Sabbih Isma Rabbika al-A'la on the night of Friday, and in the Dhuhr prayer (صلاة الظهر), al-Jumu'ah and al-Munafiqun. If he does that, it is as if he is performing the deed of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and his reward (ثوابه) and recompense (جزاؤه) upon Allah is Paradise (الجنّة).
